events_caption: Security events propagated across the mesh
indicators_caption: Indicators of compromise shared by peers
peers_caption: Registered nodes in the Nordlys mesh
health_subtitle: Real-time Varde relay health scores and metrics
vuln_tip_unresolved: New + acknowledged + in progress. Not yet mitigated.
vuln_tip_status_new: Just received via mesh. Not yet reviewed by your team.
vuln_tip_status_acknowledged: Reviewed and confirmed relevant to your systems.
vuln_tip_status_in_progress: Your team is actively applying countermeasures.
vuln_tip_status_exploited: Confirmed exploitation — you or another mesh node was compromised.
vuln_tip_local_version: How many times this node has updated its local copy. Each node maintains its own version independently.
vuln_changelog_tip: Local history of how this vulnerability record has been updated. Shows when new sources delivered data and which fields changed. Higher-trust sources override lower-trust ones.
landing_page_title: Nordlys — Shared security toolkit for the Norwegian energy sector
landing_tagline: Shared security toolkit for the Norwegian energy sector
landing_lead: A shared early-warning network for the Norwegian energy sector.
landing_eyebrow: For CISOs, SOCs, and security leadership in the energy sector
landing_problem_intro: Three conditions, each manageable on its own — together they paint a clear picture:
landing_problem_1_title: The energy sector is a targeted domain
landing_problem_1_body: Volt Typhoon. Sandworm. Industroyer. State-aligned actors prioritise power supply as a target — and when one Norwegian actor is hit, the others are likely next.
landing_problem_2_body: The NIS2 directive, Norway's security act and power-sector preparedness regulations require sector actors to notify and share relevant threat information. Which channel? What auditability? Still unanswered.
landing_problem_3_title: Today's sharing is manual and slow
landing_problem_3_body: IoCs are shared by email, Signal groups, and shared spreadsheets. No signing, no auditability, no automation. When one peer sees something, hours or days pass before the others know.
landing_what_p1: Each organisation runs its own Nordlys node locally. The node collects events from its own SIEM, scans for vulnerabilities, and shares signed findings with other peers in real time.
landing_what_p2: KraftCERT is the trust anchor that signs peer identities — and is itself a peer in the mesh. KraftCERT sees the same as every other participant. No central storage, no hidden channel.
landing_what_p3: Every share is signed by its source. The receiver can verify who said what, when. Auditability without giving up ownership.
landing_why_intro: Nordlys is built for the energy sector's real problems:
landing_why_1_body: When one peer sees an attack attempt or a new IoC, the whole mesh knows within seconds. Build detection on data from the entire sector — not just your own logs.
landing_why_2_body: NIS2, Norway's security act, and power-sector preparedness regulations expect information sharing. Nordlys provides a signed, auditable medium — no manual emails or shared spreadsheets.
landing_why_3_body: No central database. No foreign cloud service. Every organisation owns its own node. KraftCERT signs identities but never sees the payload.
landing_why_4_body: Value grows with every peer that joins. Individuals from several major energy actors are already engaged — join in and put your organisation on the map.
landing_model_intro: Nordlys is open source. There is no licence fee — but participants are expected to contribute according to capacity.
landing_model_cost_body: No cash licence. Contributions are expected proportional to size: large actors like Statnett contribute substantially through development, infrastructure, and coordination. Smaller energy companies can participate without contributing financially — the value to the community lies in their presence in the mesh.
landing_model_plugins_body: Plugin architecture based on open interfaces. Custom detections, integrations against internal SIEM/SOAR, and sector-specific modules can ship as open plugins and be shared across peers — without waiting for a central release cycle.
landing_model_open_body: All code is published on GitHub. No black box, no hidden components. Security audits and independent code review are explicitly welcome.
landing_feature_1_body: Security events from SIEM systems are signed locally and propagated to other peers via mesh.
landing_feature_2_body: Local scanning and receipt of shared vulnerabilities with CVSS scoring and status tracking.
landing_feature_3_body: Visual mesh graph with live RTT, health scoring and event route trails.
landing_feature_4_body: Indicators (IP, domain, hash) with TLP marking, signed by source.
landing_initiative_lead: Nordlys is not a corporate project — it is an open initiative driven forward by individuals working at several Norwegian energy companies:
landing_quickstart_intro: Three steps from zero to mesh — about as long as it takes to make coffee.
landing_quickstart_1_body: Docker compose up. The node runs on your own machine or VPS. ~2 GB RAM, sub-minute cold start.
landing_quickstart_2_body: The setup wizard generates a signed access request. Send it to KraftCERT via a secure channel.
landing_quickstart_3_body: Once KraftCERT approves, you receive an invitation token. Paste it — the node is live in the mesh.
landing_audience_ciso: CISOs who want a sector-wide situational picture
landing_audience_soc: SOC analysts who need validated IoCs from peers
landing_audience_ot: OT security teams focused on industry-specific threats
landing_audience_compliance: Security leadership with reporting duty under NIS2 / national security act
landing_status_body: Nordlys is in active development for a demo at TechDay 2026. We welcome feedback, code review, and pilot participants now.
landing_cta_join_body: We're happy to set up a call. Reach out via GitHub or directly to KraftCERT.
login_error_server: Could not reach the server. Check your network connection.
mesh_banner_text: Mesh connection inactive — waiting for KraftCERT approval.
integrations_apikeys_desc: API keys are used by SIEM systems and scanners to send data to the node. Keys are configured for now via the API_KEYS environment variable.
integrations_apikeys_placeholder: Managing API keys via the UI is coming in the next version.
integrations_docs_desc: Interactive OpenAPI documentation (Swagger UI) for the full Nordlys API — endpoints, payloads, examples, and a built-in test console.
general_identity_desc: Permanent values for this node. Set during initial setup and cannot be changed afterwards.
general_node_name_hint: Used only for display in the dashboard.
auth_local_desc: Manage users who sign in with email and password.
auth_local_placeholder: User management is coming in the next version.
auth_oidc_desc: Connect the node to your organisation's identity provider for single sign-on.
auth_oidc_placeholder: OIDC integration is coming in the next version.
apikeys_desc: API keys are used by SIEM systems and scanners to send data to the node. Keys are configured for now via the API_KEYS environment variable.
apikeys_placeholder: Managing API keys via the UI is coming in the next version.
mesh_hub_desc: This node is the KraftCERT hub — trust anchor for the mesh network. Provisioning new peers (approving access requests, peer list, revocations) lives on its own page.
mesh_manual_invite_desc: Issue an invitation directly without a signed access request. Only used for testing or when the peer node cannot generate a request itself.
mesh_invite_hint: Deliver the token to the peer node via a secure channel. The token is single-use.
mesh_access_request_desc: Generate a signed request to deliver to KraftCERT to register the node in the mesh network.
mesh_request_hint: Download or copy this JSON and deliver it to KraftCERT via a secure channel.
mesh_redeem_desc: Once KraftCERT has approved the request, you'll receive an invitation token in return.
mesh_redeem_success: The node is registered in the mesh network.
peers_only_kraftcert: This page is only available on the KraftCERT node.
peers_approve_desc: Paste a signed access request received from the peer node. The signature is verified against the included public key, and an invitation bound to the stated company is issued.
peers_placeholder_access_request: Paste JSON from the peer node…
peers_token_hint: Deliver the token to the peer node via the same channel the request came from.
peers_revoke_dialog_warning: The revocation is signed and distributed via mesh. The action cannot be undone.
peers_error_invalid_json: Could not parse JSON. Make sure the entire request was pasted.
revocations_desc: All signed revocations issued by this node. Revocations propagate via mesh and invalidate the named peer identity.
revocations_error_load: Could not load the revocation log
about_text: Nordlys is developed for secure information sharing between Norwegian energy companies under coordination by KraftCERT.
setup_hint_org: Full organisation name. Cannot be changed after setup.
setup_hint_node_name: Short identifier for this node (e.g. department, location, function). Cannot be changed.
setup_verify_desc: To verify you control this server, run the following command in the terminal:
setup_verify_hint: The code is valid for 1 hour. Maximum 5 attempts.
setup_access_desc: Your node is ready. To connect to the mesh network, KraftCERT must approve access.
setup_access_step_2: Send to your contact at KraftCERT via a secure channel (email, Signal)
setup_access_step_3: Wait for an invitation code in return via the same channel
setup_access_step_4_html: Go to Settings → Mesh and paste the code to activate
setup_access_works_now_html: What works now? Events, vulnerability scanning, and local analysis are available immediately. Sharing with other nodes in the mesh network is activated after KraftCERT approval.
setup_node_preview: How the node will appear in the mesh network
